headline: Anthropic's release of Claude Fable 5 and the Mythos family dominated the window with a wave of agentic capability demonstrations.
body: Anthropic released the Mythos model family alongside Fable 5, described as Mythos 5 with guardrails and offered at no extra cost on Pro, Max, Team, and Enterprise plans through June 22 before shifting to usage credits. The launch drove a cluster of capability demos: Fable 5 autonomously played Factorio, simulated the solar system from physics first principles to predict a solar eclipse, set a fluid simulation to a Beethoven remix, and designed a 3D-printable model in a CAD editor it built itself. Physics simulation, reasoning, and game playing all broke out on the back of these demonstrations, while GPU clusters held a leading position via RunPod's IDE-based deployment framework. Coverage skewed toward promotional channels, with some videos overstating the model's significance.
